steps.title: Find Victor's core complaint
steps.content: The strongest clue is Victor's own assessment: he saw “fortifications and impediments” keeping humans from entering nature's “citadel” and “repined” (expressed discontent) about them. This reveals his belief that conventional approaches face fundamental barriers to deep understanding.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer choices
steps.content: D is correct: Victor's frustration signals his conviction that conventional science will not yield the answers he seeks—the passage's emphasis on science's inability to understand underlying causes and penetrate nature's mysteries confirms this view. A, B, and C all contradict this by claiming science offers insight or advantage. That matches choice D — D is correct.
